attributes.name: LD_PRELOAD Persistence File Write - /etc/ld.preload
attributes.description: Detects file creation or modification of /etc/ld.preload or /etc/ld.so.preload, the primary persistence mechanism for LD_PRELOAD-based rootkits. Auto-Color writes malicious library paths to /etc/ld.preload, WolfsBane (Gelsemium APT) uses /etc/ld.so.preload with a modified BEURK rootkit, and Perfctl uses both as persistence fallbacks. These files are almost never legitimately modified.
attributes.query: file where event.type in ("creation", "change") and file.path in ("/etc/ld.preload", "/etc/ld.so.preload")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Extremely rare - these files are almost never used in legitimate configurations

attributes.name: Suspicious Shared Library Drop in System Directory
attributes.description: Detects creation of shared library (.so) files in system library directories by non-package-manager processes. Perfctl drops libgcwrap.so into /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/, Symbiote deploys rootkit .so files to system paths, and Auto-Color installs malicious libraries referenced by /etc/ld.preload. Files in /dev/shm/ and /tmp/ are especially suspicious.
attributes.query: file where event.type == "creation" and file.extension == "so" and file.path : ("/lib/*", "/usr/lib/*", "/lib64/*", "/usr/lib64/*", "/usr/local/lib/*", "/dev/shm/*", "/var/tmp/*", "/tmp/*") and not process.name in ("dpkg", "rpm", "apt", "apt-get", "yum", "dnf", "zypper", "pacman", "ldconfig", "make", "install")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

attributes.name: Systemd Service Created Outside Package Management
attributes.description: Detects creation of systemd service unit files in standard systemd directories by processes other than package managers. Perfctl creates systemd services with LD_PRELOAD environment variables to persist its malicious shared library across reboots, using deceptive names like 'System Guard Daemon'.
attributes.query: file where event.type == "creation" and file.path : ("/etc/systemd/system/*.service", "/lib/systemd/system/*.service", "/usr/lib/systemd/system/*.service", "/run/systemd/system/*.service") and not process.name in ("dpkg", "rpm", "apt", "apt-get", "yum", "dnf")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Legitimate software installation creating custom services
Configuration management tools (Ansible, Puppet) deploying services

attributes.name: PAM Module Directory Modification - Credential Hooking
attributes.description: Detects file creation or modification in PAM module directories (/etc/pam.d/, /lib/security/) by non-package-manager processes. Perfctl installs a malicious PAM module that hooks pam_authenticate to intercept credentials from sshd, sudo, and login services before forwarding them to the legitimate PAM stack.
attributes.query: file where event.type in ("creation", "change") and file.path : ("/etc/pam.d/*", "/lib/security/*", "/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/security/*", "/lib64/security/*", "/usr/lib/security/*") and not process.name in ("dpkg", "rpm", "apt", "apt-get", "yum", "dnf", "pam-auth-update")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

attributes.name: Suspicious /etc/shadow Access - Credential Dumping
attributes.description: Detects access to /etc/shadow by processes that do not normally read the shadow password file. Perfctl reads /etc/shadow after gaining root access to harvest password hashes for offline cracking. Crackable hashes (SHA-512 $6$, yescrypt $y$) are staged for exfiltration.
attributes.query: file where event.type == "access" and file.path == "/etc/shadow" and not process.name in ("passwd", "chpasswd", "useradd", "usermod", "userdel", "login", "sshd", "cron", "crond", "sudo", "su", "nscd", "sssd", "groupadd", "groupmod", "newusers")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Security auditing tools checking shadow file permissions

attributes.name: SUID Binary Enumeration via find - Privilege Escalation Recon
attributes.description: Detects find commands searching for SUID/SGID binaries, a standard privilege escalation reconnaissance step. Perfctl and other Linux malware enumerate SUID binaries to discover exploitable GTFOBins entries (find, vim, bash, nmap, etc.) that provide root shell access without password authentication.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name == "find" and process.args : ("-perm", "-u=s", "/4000", "-4000", "+4000", "/6000", "-6000")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

attributes.name: SUID Bit Set on System Interpreter - Critical Privilege Escalation
attributes.description: Detects chmod commands setting the SUID bit on interpreter binaries (bash, sh, python, perl, vim, find, nmap). Setting SUID on an interpreter creates a persistent root shell accessible by any local user. Perfctl uses 'chmod u+s /bin/bash' after initial root compromise to maintain permanent privilege escalation.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name == "chmod" and process.args : ("u+s", "4755", "4711", "4777", "4775") and process.args : ("/bin/bash", "/bin/sh", "/usr/bin/bash", "/usr/bin/sh", "/usr/bin/python*", "/usr/bin/perl", "/usr/bin/vim*", "/usr/bin/find", "/usr/bin/nmap", "/usr/bin/env")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Extremely rare - setting SUID on interpreters is almost never legitimate

attributes.name: GTFOBins SUID Exploitation - Shell Escape Pattern
attributes.description: Detects known GTFOBins SUID exploitation patterns: find -exec shell, vim -c shell escape, bash -p privileged mode, nmap --interactive, and python os.setuid calls. These patterns indicate active privilege escalation via misconfigured SUID binaries on the system.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and (process.args : ("-exec", "/bin/sh", "/bin/bash") and process.name == "find" or process.name : "vim*" and process.args : ("-c", ":!/bin/sh", ":shell") or process.name == "bash" and process.args == "-p" or process.name == "nmap" and process.args == "--interactive" or process.name : "python*" and process.args : "*os.setuid*")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Some find -exec patterns in legitimate file management

attributes.name: Crontab Binary Modified - Potential Trojanization
attributes.description: Detects modification or replacement of the system crontab binary outside of package management. Perfctl replaces the crontab binary with a trojanized version that filters malicious cron entries from 'crontab -l' output while the entries remain active in the raw spool files (/var/spool/cron/).
attributes.query: file where event.type in ("change", "creation") and file.path : ("/usr/bin/crontab", "/usr/sbin/crontab", "/bin/crontab", "/sbin/crontab") and not process.name in ("dpkg", "rpm", "apt", "apt-get", "yum", "dnf")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

attributes.name: Cryptomining Pool DNS Query - Perfctl Cryptojacking
attributes.description: Detects DNS queries to known cryptocurrency mining pool domains. Perfctl deploys XMRig for Monero mining, connecting to supportxmr.com, xmrpool.eu, hashvault.pro, and other mining pools. Mining pool connections from servers are a strong indicator of cryptojacking compromise.
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Legitimate cryptocurrency mining operations (should not exist on production servers)

attributes.name: Known Perfctl Watchdog Process Execution
attributes.description: Detects execution of processes with names associated with the Perfctl malware family, including the watchdog binary 'wizlmsh' and 'perfctl' itself. These processes monitor persistence mechanisms and restart the cryptomining payload if it is terminated.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and (process.name in ("wizlmsh", "perfctl", "perfcc") or process.args : ("wizlmsh", "perfctl", "--check-persist"))
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: None expected - these are known malware process names

attributes.name: LD_PRELOAD Attack Chain Correlation - Multi-Stage Compromise
attributes.description: Detects the correlated LD_PRELOAD attack chain: shared library creation followed by SUID binary enumeration on the same host within 1 hour. This sequence mirrors the Perfctl/Symbiote/Auto-Color attack lifecycle from initial persistence through privilege escalation.
attributes.query: sequence by host.id with maxspan=1h [file where event.type == "creation" and file.extension == "so" and file.path : ("/lib/*", "/usr/lib/*", "/tmp/*", "/dev/shm/*") and not process.name in ("dpkg", "rpm", "apt", "yum", "dnf", "ldconfig")] [process where event.type == "start" and process.name == "find" and process.args : ("-perm", "-u=s", "/4000", "-4000")]
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Extremely unlikely when both indicators occur on the same host within 1 hour
